rationale: The citing paper studies food intake/calorie choices and healthy-food framing in some experiments, so the cited field paper on advance ordering and lower lunch calories could plausibly be relevant background in that food-choice area. But the citing note’s core mechanism is robot-induced eeriness/identity threat, with no explicit link to order-timing mechanisms from the cited study, and the raw evidence is only a DOI match without in-text context. With this limited evidence, it is not safe to drop as obvious mention-only nor clearly keep as substantive.
